solvents.name: Bacteriostatic Water (BAC)
solvents.notes: Contains 0.9% benzyl alcohol as preservative. Standard for multi-use vials.

solvents.name: Sterile Water
solvents.notes: No preservative. Single use only. Discard remainder.

syringes.notes: Half-size insulin syringe. Same unit scale, smaller barrel.

bpc-157.notes: Trickle water down side of vial, don't inject directly onto powder.

semaglutide.notes: Pre-filled pens don't require reconstitution. Only compounded powder vials need mixing.

tirzepatide.notes: Compounded vials vary widely. Check concentration label (mg/mL) before calculating.

retatrutide.notes: May take longer to dissolve. Roll gently, do not shake.

tb-500.notes: Fragile peptide bond. Temperature sensitivity higher than most. Keep cold.

cjc-1295.notes: Often sold with or without DAC. Check label.

ipamorelin.notes: Commonly blended with CJC-1295. If blended, reconstitute as one.

ghk-cu.notes: Also available as topical cream/serum. Only SubQ vials require reconstitution.

tesamorelin.notes: FDA version (Egrifta) comes with specific diluent.

thymosin-alpha-1.notes: Often pre-dosed at 1.6mg per vial for single injection.

semax.notes: Intranasal. Usually comes as pre-mixed nasal spray.
